The Leather Whip is a pre-Hardmode whip that is normally always available to be be purchased from the Zoologist for 10 GC (except in certain scenarios ). It has a 50% multihit penalty and performs the following effects on hit:

The Blend-O-Matic is a crafting station that is purchased from the Steampunker for 10 GC . On the Old-gen console and 3DS , it can only be purchased at night. Its sole purpose is to craft Asphalt Blocks which are used to run faster when walked on.

conditions: Blinkroot is a type of herb that appears as stems with glowing dots. It grows naturally on Dirt or Mud Blocks , regardless of lighting or elevation, as long as the blocks bear no grass of any kind. Blinkroot can be harvested with virtually any weapon or tool , and can be planted manually using Blinkroot Seeds in Clay Pots and Planter Boxes , or on suitable soil tiles .

Blinkroot Seeds are a type of seed that can be obtained from harvesting Blinkroots while they are blooming or using the Staff of Regrowth on naturally grown Blinkroots while they are fully grown. A Blinkroot plant switches between its blooming and non-blooming state at random. Its blooming state can be identified by the yellow dots on the ends of its fronds and the blinking light it emits. Each plant will drop 1–3 Blinkroot Seeds when harvested while blooming.

The Blizzard in a Bottle is a pre-Hardmode accessory which works similarly to the Cloud in a Bottle , allowing the player to double-jump. Instead of a cloud being produced when the player jumps, a trail of snow is produced at the player's feet. The Blizzard jump goes 8 tiles into the air, totaling 14 tiles when put together with the first jump. The Blizzard in a Bottle has a 15.52% chance of being found inside Frozen Chests in the Ice biome , as well as a 1/6 (16.67%) chance of being obtained from Frozen (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) and Boreal Crates (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) . On the Desktop version , Console version , and Mobile version , it can also be crafted at a Crystal Ball .

notes: Normal mode row
Bloodbath Dye is a dye purchased from the Dye Trader during a Blood Moon for 7 GC 50 SC . When applied, it gives the item an appearance similar to dripping blood.
sourceRefName: Dye Trader during a Blood Moon for

notes: Normal mode row

The Blowgun is a pre-Hardmode ranged weapon that can be purchased from the Witch Doctor for 5 GC . It is an indirect upgrade to the Blowpipe . When used, it fires Seeds and darts as ammunition . While a Blowgun is in a player's inventory, there is a chance that any vegetation growing on normal grass , in Clay Pots , and in Planter Boxes (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) will drop Seeds. Flowers typically drop 4 Seeds at a time, while tall grass typically drops 1–2 Seeds.

conditions: The Blowpipe is a pre-Hardmode ranged weapon that can found randomly in Wooden Chests near the Surface or in the Underground layer . When used, it fires Seeds and darts as ammunition . While a Blowpipe is in a player's inventory, there is a chance that any vegetation growing on normal grass , in Clay Pots , and in Planter Boxes will drop Seeds. Flowers typically drop 4 Seeds at a time, while tall grass typically drops 1–2 Seeds.

Blue Berries are a dye material and type of plant . Placed Blue Berries have the appearance of a blueberry bush. They can grow on regular grass or Jungle grass above surface . [ 1 ] Unlike many plants, instead of broken by any attack, it has to be harvested with a pickaxe . Its sole purpose is to make Blue Dye .

notes: The Blue Chicken Egg is a pet-summoning item that spawns a Blue Chicken that follows the player. The Blue Chicken Egg can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 25 GC .

The Bomb can be purchased from the Demolitionist and Skeleton Merchant for 3 SC each or obtained from Pots , Chests , slimes , by powering Bomb Statues , or as a drop from Undead Miners with a 18/25 (72%) chance. It has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from slimes.

The Bone Arrow is a pre-Hardmode arrow purchased from the Skeleton Merchant for 15 CC each, during moon phases 1 (full), 2, 5 (new), and 6. Bone Arrows can pierce one enemy .

conditions: The Book is a crafting material used only to craft Bookcases , Pianos , and Librarian Blocks . It can also be placed as decoration on Flat-surface items . Books are found placed on the Dungeon 's many shelves, where they can be freed with a pickaxe or drill . They can also be renewably obtained from Dungeon or Stockade Crates , and can be bought from the Wizard for 15 SC each.

notes: Normal mode row

conditions: The Boomstick is a pre-Hardmode gun . It can be found in Ivy Chests ( 21.72% chance) in the Underground Jungle , and also in Jungle Crates (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) ( 19/100 (19%) chance). When used, it fires a spread of 3–4 bullets , consuming one piece of ammunition .

notes: Booster Tracks are placeable furniture -type blocks that can be placed next to Minecart Tracks to automatically boost the speed of a Minecart that rides over it. The direction of the boost is indicated by the animated red arrow it displays, which can be toggled using a Hammer or wire . Each Booster Track increases speed by 20 mph , regardless of the Minecart variant used. The Booster Track can be purchased from the Mechanic for 50 SC .

conditions: Boreal Wood is a type of wood harvested from Boreal trees found naturally growing in the Snow biome , or from Underground Cabins in the Ice biome . Boreal trees can be cultivated by placing Acorns on top of Snow Blocks above surface .

Boreal Wood is a type of wood harvested from Boreal trees found naturally growing in the Snow biome , or from Underground Cabins in the Ice biome . Boreal trees can be cultivated by placing Acorns on top of Snow Blocks above surface .

Bottled Honey is a pre-Hardmode crafting material and recovery potion . When used, it restores 80 health and inflicts Potion Sickness for 60 seconds. This duration can be reduced via the Philosopher's Stone or Charm of Myths . It also grants the player the Honey buff for 15 seconds. In addition to being crafted, Bottled Honey is also dropped by the Queen Bee , found in Bee Hives in the Underground Jungle .

notes: The Brick Layer is a construction accessory that is purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 10 GC . While equipped, it grants +50% placement speed when placing blocks .
Normal mode row
Bubbles are purchased from the Party Girl for 2 SC each during Hardmode .

The Bubble Machine is a novelty furniture mechanism purchased from the Party Girl for 4 GC (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) during the day / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) at any time. When placed, it continuously produces bubbles. These bubbles are purely visual, have no functional effect, and can be produced infinitely at no cost.

The Bubble Wand is an auto-swing novelty item that produces rainbow-colored bubbles. The bubbles are purely visual, have no functional effect, and can be produced infinitely at no cost. The Bubble Wand is purchased from the Party Girl for 5 GC .
The Bug Net can be purchased from the Merchant for (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) 25 SC / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) 1 GC .

The Bunny Cannon is a Hardmode placeable furniture / weapon . It can be purchased from the Pirate for 50 GC when the Party Girl is present and one of the mechanical bosses has been defeated. Once placed, it can be fired by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack key on it with any Torch (which is not consumed). The Bunny Cannon fires an arcing Explosive Bunny projectile that does not destroy blocks and pierces an infinite amount of targets. After the projectile loses all its momentum, it will (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) transform into an Explosive Bunny critter / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) explode after several seconds.
Normal mode row

quantityText: Butterfly Dust ( (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) Only after one mechanical boss has been defeated / (Old-gen console and 3DS versions) At any time)

conditions: The cactus plant grows in Desert biomes on all types of Sand . Cactus plants can be harvested using an axe or chainsaw , which causes Cactus items to drop. Axe power determines the number of hits it takes to destroy a cactus tile. The Sand Block the cactus plant grows on is unbreakable and not affected by gravity. Putting a sand block up to one of the stems, right under it, and then cutting the main cactus body, will make the branch a new cactus. Corruption , Crimson , and Hallow cacti will all drop the same Cactus items when harvested.

notes: Normal mode row

The Cannon is a Hardmode placeable furniture / weapon , purchased from the Pirate for 25 GC . Once placed, it can be fired by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack key on it with any Torch (which is not consumed). The Cannon fires an arcing Cannonball that explodes upon contact with a block or after piercing three enemies . The explosion does not destroy blocks.
The Cannonball is a type of decorative block sold by the Pirate . Cannonballs can be purchased from the Pirate for 2 SC each.

conditions: The Can Of Worms is a consumable grab bag item, which when opened with ⚷ Open / Activate from within the player's inventory , contains 5–11 different worms. Cans Of Worms can be found in Chests on the surface .

The Bast Statue is a furniture item that grants The Bast Defense buff to players in its vicinity, increasing their defense by 5. The buff effect can be toggled via wire . The Bast Statue can be found in Sandstone Chests in the lower 3/7ths of the Underground Desert and in Oasis Crates and Mirage Crates obtained from fishing in the Oasis . It can also be found as a furniture in Underground Desert Cabins .

notes: The Celestial Wand is a pet-summoning item that spawns Estee to follow the player. When the player is far enough away, Estee will emit a bright yellow beam trail while flying at high speed in order to catch up to them, which resembles that of a Fallen Star . The Celestial Wand can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 1 PC , and is always sold alongside the Star Princess set .
Normal mode row

conditions: Chaos Fish are a type of fish which are found rarely by fishing in the Underground Hallow . They are used to craft Seafood Dinners (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) and Teleportation Potions .

notes: The Christmas Tree is a decorative Hardmode furniture item purchased from Santa Claus for 25 SC . It can be decorated with toppers, garlands, bulbs, and lights to alter its appearance.
Normal mode row

conditions: Clay is a block type mainly used as a crafting material . It is found primarily in Surface -level (above 0 elevation) areas, and is more rare at the upper parts of the Underground . Because of this, the largest deposits of Clay can be found simply by exploring the Surface for differently-colored patches in the Dirt .

notes: It can be purchased from the Steampunker for 2 PC .
